novel_features.name: Price-drop watch
novel_features.description: Snapshot a location's deals now and get told which rooms dropped below your threshold or fell since you last looked.
novel_features.rationale: Requires diffing the current inventory against a local time-series of past observations for the same geo and date; HotelTonight has no watchlist, alert, or price memory.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs to monitor a city for last-minute hotel price drops over time instead of re-querying blind.

novel_features.name: Price history
novel_features.description: Show the recorded price and % off history for one hotel from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Reads the price_snapshots time-series from SQLite; the app deliberately erases prior prices, so this history exists nowhere else.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you need the actual price trajectory of a hotel rather than a single ephemeral quote.

novel_features.name: Is-this-a-deal verdict
novel_features.description: Classify a hotel's current quoted price against its own observed low/median/high as cheap, typical, or expensive.
novel_features.rationale: Computes percentile bands from the hotel's stored snapshot history — an objective baseline the app never surfaces. Mechanical math, no model.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to answer 'is this actually cheap?' with a grounded baseline instead of a guess.

novel_features.name: Neighborhood compare
novel_features.description: Group tonight's deals in a metro by neighborhood and rank the neighborhoods by median price or best % off.
novel_features.rationale: A local group-by over inventory rows; the flat /v6/inventory feed returns no neighborhood rollup and the app shows one pin at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when deciding which area of a city has the best last-minute value tonight.

novel_features.name: Date-shift scan
novel_features.description: Compare a location's deals across tonight, tomorrow, and the weekend in one ranked side-by-side view.
novel_features.rationale: Fans out real inventory calls per date and assembles a comparison the app only shows one date at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find the cheapest night to stay in an area without running the search repeatedly.

novel_features.name: Daily Drop tracker
novel_features.description: Reveal today's Daily Drop hotel and its real discounted price for a market (the app hides it behind a slide-to-unlock gate), and with --history read the recorded run of past Daily Drops.
novel_features.rationale: The Daily Drop is gated behind an account-only unlock in the app, but the full deal (hotel, price, savings) is embedded in the server-rendered results page; this reads it anonymously and persists it, recording the once-a-day deal the app makes ephemeral.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to see and track HotelTonight's exclusive once-a-day flash deal, which the app hides until you unlock it and erases each day.

narrative.headline: Last-minute hotel deals with a local price-history database no HotelTonight client has — snapshot deals over time, watch a city for drops, and rank by real % off.
narrative.value_prop: HotelTonight's deals are deliberately ephemeral and geo-local: they appear, drop, and vanish, and you only ever see now, here. This CLI syncs the anonymous deal feed into a local SQLite store and snapshots prices over time, so you (or an agent) can watch a city for drops, see a hotel's real price history, and get an objective cheap/typical/expensive verdict — none of which the app supports.
narrative.auth_narrative: No account or API key. HotelTonight's consumer endpoints are reachable anonymously; the CLI sends the app-identifying headers it needs automatically. Booking still happens in the app — this tool is read-only.

quickstart.comment: See HotelTonight's major markets and their ids (1 = San Francisco, 72 = Austin).
Pull tonight's deals near a location ranked by % off; this also records a price snapshot so history and watch have data to work with.
Flag rooms under $150 or that dropped since the last snapshot.

troubleshoots.symptom: inventory search returns an error about a bad request
troubleshoots.fix: Pass both --latitude and --longitude; the deal feed is geo-anchored and needs a coordinate pair.

troubleshoots.symptom: history or verdict says no data for a hotel
troubleshoots.fix: Run sync for that area first — those commands read the local snapshot store, which starts empty.

troubleshoots.symptom: a market id is not in 'markets list'
troubleshoots.fix: The list is a curated set of major markets; any numeric id still resolves via 'markets get <id>', and search works from any lat/lng.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or script needs last-minute hotel deal data from HotelTonight that persists and compounds: monitoring a city for price drops, building a price history for a hotel, judging whether a quoted rate is actually cheap, or comparing neighborhoods and dates. It is read-only and anonymous — it never books and never needs a login.

recipes.title: Tonight's cheapest near a coordinate, trimmed for agents
recipes.explanation: The raw inventory payload is tens of KB of nested deal data; --select with dotted paths returns just the hotel, neighborhood, price, 30-day-high, and deal type so an agent doesn't burn context.

recipes.title: Watch a neighborhood for a price target
recipes.explanation: Snapshots current deals and reports rooms now under $150 or cheaper than the last observation — run it on a cron to catch drops while you're away.

recipes.title: Decide which night to stay
recipes.explanation: Lines up tonight vs tomorrow vs the weekend for the same area so you can pick the cheapest night in one view.

recipes.title: Is this rate actually good?
recipes.explanation: Classifies the current quote against the hotel's own recorded low/median/high — requires a few prior syncs to build the baseline.

trigger_phrases: watch this city for hotel price drops
is this hotel price a good deal
what's the daily drop in San Francisco
